hypothesis: If embeddings from diverse scientific foundation models are compared on shared molecules and materials using CKNNA, IDCor, and information imbalance, then the models will exhibit high cross-model alignment and similar intrinsic dimensions—strongest in-distribution and weakest for out-of-distribution molecules and language-only models—because shared physical symmetries and data manifolds drive convergence to a common low-dimensional representation of matter irrespective of architecture or modality.
